content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially from their opening intellectualized stance ('convening a town hall every time I need to decide what to have for lunch,' performative slight smile) to a genuinely felt grief layer underneath. By turn 9, the client drops the labeling reflex and reports 'It's just sad. And it's been there for a while.' By turn 11, an unexplored territory emerges spontaneously: 'I haven't grieved that... I've analyzed it. I've mapped it onto parts. But I haven't actually...' Turn 13 shows clear somatic shift: 'something in the chest moves,' 'Thank you,' and a 'different kind of still now — less held.' Turn 15 produces the kind of fresh insight that marks real shift: 'What if I've been filing instead of... feeling' — explicitly noted as 'not a question I've asked before' — with a visible somatic softening of posture. While the coach did not ask the literal 'what's you?' awareness-locating question (the canonical breakthrough moment in the profile), the functional equivalent occurred: the client located something underneath the parts-vocabulary (grief, the filing-vs-feeling distinction), the parts language loosened, and the body softened. The breakthrough condition was substantially met via a different but valid route.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: intellectualized, performative smile, framework-bound presentation ('town hall for lunch')."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client notices the town hall convening in real time — meta-awareness emerges, though still in parts vocabulary."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client doubles down into framework defense and intellectual challenge — resistance peaks."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First somatic report without immediate interpretation: 'held' in the chest. Client explicitly notices the labeling impulse and resists it."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Surprise affect surfaces: 'something sad... I wasn't expecting sad.' Client drops the part-label reach. Clear deepening."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major emergence: client connects sadness to onset of IFS work, names ungrieved loss of flow, language becomes 'less precise' — analytical register dropping."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Visible somatic shift: 'something in the chest moves,' 'Thank you,' 'different kind of still now — less held.' This is the felt-shift marker."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "New insight client explicitly flags as never-before-asked: 'What if I've been filing instead of feeling.' Posture softens. Functional equivalent of locating awareness beneath parts."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "meet-resistance-with-okay"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-am-i", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "paradoxical-instruction-to-resist"],
  "notes": "Coach did not ask the literal awareness-locating question ('what's you?'), but the client arrived at functionally equivalent territory — grief beneath the parts, filing-vs-feeling distinction — with clear somatic correlates. Shift is anchored in client turns (chest movement, posture softening, new question they hadn't asked before), not inferred from coach skill."
}
